texts.full_text: provided background and methods: "Background: Peracetic acid (PAA) has antimicrobial activity against bacteria and fungi, including spores, and is environmentally safe. Despite its widespread use in the food industry and effluent treatment, it is not commonly used in hospitals. The present study aimed to evaluate the efficacy of PAA against hospital pathogens, including bacterial and fungal spores and mycobacteria. Methods: Multidrug-resistant, wild hospital isolates of Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria, as well as tough organisms such as Mycobacterium fortuitum, Mycobacterium tuberculosis, and Candida albicans, were tested using a modified Kelsey-Sykes suspension test. For vegetative bacteria, the exposure time was 1 minute. For bacterial and fungal spores, including Candida

texts.full_text: About Restaurant Kyoto Sushi views on this website have been left by actual guests whilKyoto Sushi reviews on this website have been left by actual guests while you can explore our diverse menu, featuring the freshest ingredients and authentic Japanese flavors. Discover more about our selection of sushi and Japanese cuisine, and experience the artistry behind each creation. We invite you to come and taste the difference at Kyoto Sushi, where quality meets tradItion.
